sources: ACSM Position Stand: Exercise and Fluid Replacement (Med Sci Sports Exerc, 2007)
note: General exercise population guidelines. Consult a sports dietitian for endurance athletes.

before_exercise.purpose: Ensure euhydration (normal fluid balance) before training

during_exercise.under_60_min: Water sufficient for most people doing moderate exercise
during_exercise.over_60_min: Add electrolytes (sodium, potassium) if sweating heavily; 150–250 ml every 15–20 min
during_exercise.over_90_min_or_heavy_sweat: Consider sports drink or electrolyte + carb combination

after_exercise.rule: 1.5 L water per kg of body weight lost (weigh before/after for precision)
after_exercise.practical_note: If you don't weigh yourself: drink until urine is pale yellow
after_exercise.with_food: Eating post-workout meal with normal sodium content naturally restores electrolytes

sodium.role: Primary electrolyte lost in sweat; regulates fluid balance
sodium.exercise_note: Light exercisers usually get enough from food. Relevant mainly for endurance/heavy sweating.

potassium.role: Muscle contraction, fluid balance, reduces cramping

magnesium.role: Muscle function, energy metabolism, sleep quality and recovery
magnesium.exercise_note: Magnesium is often depleted in active individuals — worth tracking in vegetarian athletes.

signs_of_dehydration_to_flag: Muscle cramps during or after exercise
Feeling dizzy or lightheaded during exercise
